explanation: Your belly looks or feels swollen, larger than usual, or tight. It may be due to gas, fluid, or digestive issues.
Skin looks different than normal — color changes, rashes, lumps, scaling, or sores that weren't there before.
Your tongue has unusual color (white, red, black), coating, swelling, cracks, or smooth patches.
You or someone hears wheezing, crackling, stridor (high-pitched sound), or gurgling when you breathe.
You jerk, twitch, shake, or make movements you cannot control, like tremors or tics.
Your eyelid twitches, flutters, droops, or closes without you meaning to.

symptom_name: abnormal size or shape of ear
explanation: One or both ears look larger, smaller, misshapen, or different than before or compared to other people.
You have missed three or more periods in a row when not pregnant, breastfeeding, or menopausal.
You drink more than intended, can't cut down, or it causes problems at work, home, or health.
You feel a dull, generalized pain in muscles or joints throughout your entire body, not just one spot.
Red bumps, whiteheads, blackheads, or pus-filled spots on face, chest, or back.
After contact with a trigger (food, pollen, medicine), you have hives, itching, swelling, or trouble breathing.
You feel soreness, ache, or sharp pain in or around your ankle joint.
Your ankle feels hard to move fully — bending up, down, or side to side — often worse in the morning.
Your ankle looks puffy, larger, or the skin feels stretched; pressing may leave a dent.
Your ankle feels unstable, gives way easily, or you cannot push off your foot normally when walking.
You repeatedly ignore others' rights, lie, fight, show no guilt, or act impulsively without caring about rules.
You feel worried, restless, tense, or on edge most days, often with racing heart or sweating.
Breathing stops briefly during sleep (often noticed by snoring, gasping, or waking up tired).
Your arm muscle suddenly tightens painfully and may feel hard to the touch.
A bump or swelling on your arm that wasn't there before; can be soft, hard, movable, or fixed.
Any discomfort in your arm from shoulder to fingers — aching, sharp, burning, or throbbing.
Your arm feels hard to straighten or bend fully; muscles feel tight or resistant.
One or both arms look bigger, puffy, or have shiny skin, sometimes with reduced motion.
You cannot lift, hold, or move your arm with normal strength; may feel heavy or limp.
Muscles in your back suddenly knot up painfully; you may feel a hard lump of muscle.
Any bump, swelling, or growth on your back that feels different from the surrounding area.
Soreness or ache anywhere from your neck to tailbone; may be dull, sharp, or burning.
Your back feels rigid, hard to twist or bend; may loosen with movement or worsen after rest.
Visible puffiness or enlargement on your back, possibly with redness or warmth.
Your back muscles feel unable to support you, causing slouching, difficulty standing straight, or fatigue.
Involuntary urination during sleep in someone past toilet-training age (over 5-6 years).
A growth or lump felt in the bladder area (usually detected by imaging, not self-palpable).
Blood coming out of your ear canal, often after injury, infection, or eardrum rupture.
Blood visible on white part of eye (subconjunctival hemorrhage) or from inside the eye.
Gums bleed when brushing, flossing, or eating; may be pink on toothbrush or in spit.
Blood from tongue, cheeks, roof of mouth, or gums not due to obvious injury.
Fluid (clear, bloody, milky, green) coming from nipple without squeezing; can affect men too.
Complete or near-complete loss of vision in one or both eyes, not correctable with glasses.
Lumps of dark or bright red blood bigger than a dime passed during your period.
Red blood on toilet paper, in toilet bowl, or black/tarry stools indicating digested blood.
Pink, red, or cola-colored urine; you may see clots or only microscope detects it.
Deep, dull ache in bones, not muscles or joints; may be worse at night or with pressure.
Legs curve outward at knees (bowlegged) or inward so knees touch but ankles don't (knock-kneed).
More than 20 breaths per minute in an adult at rest; may feel like can't catch breath.
Small raised spots on shaft, head, or foreskin; may be pimples, warts, or normal pearly penile papules.
Hot, fiery pain in stomach area, often after meals or when stomach empty; may improve with antacids.
Heat-like discomfort behind breastbone, often from acid reflux (heartburn) or inflammation.

symptom_name: change in skin mole size or color
explanation: A mole grows, changes shape, becomes multicolored (black, red, blue), or gets irregular borders.
Stool becomes narrow, pencil-thin, very hard/soft, oily, floating, or changes color (clay, black, red).
Feeling of pressure, squeezing, or a band around your chest, making deep breathing hard.
Feeling cold with shivering or shaking, even in a warm room; sometimes with fever.
The normally clear front part of eye (cornea) or lens looks white, gray, or hazy.
Feeling of mucus or fluid in your lungs/chest, often with cough or rattling sound when breathing.
Fewer than 3 bowel movements per week, hard/dry stool, straining, or feeling of incomplete emptying.
Medical term for head cold symptoms: runny nose, sneezing, mild nasal congestion.
Sudden expulsion of air from lungs to clear airways; can be dry (no mucus) or wet (with phlegm).
Bringing up mucus/phlegm from lungs by coughing; color may be clear, white, yellow, green, or brown.
Sudden, involuntary, painful muscle tightening that can last seconds to minutes.
Eyes do not align properly — one turns inward, outward, up, or down while other looks straight.
No desire to eat, eating much less than normal, or feeling full after few bites.
Resting heart rate below 60 beats per minute in adults (unless you are athletic).
Believing things not real (delusions) or seeing/hearing/feeling things that aren't there (hallucinations).
Persistent low mood, loss of interest, hopelessness, sleep/appetite changes for over 2 weeks.
Combination of depression with psychosis — e.g., severe sadness plus false beliefs or hallucinations.
Red, inflamed skin in diaper area (buttocks, genitals, inner thighs) in infants or incontinent adults.
Loose, watery stools three or more times per day; may be with urgency or cramping.
Feeling of not getting enough air, breathlessness, or labored breathing; also called dyspnea.
Trouble chewing, swallowing, keeping food in mouth, or pain when eating.
Feeling that food or liquid sticks in throat or chest; may cough or choke when swallowing.
Slurred, slow, or jumbled speech; trouble finding words; or hoarseness affecting communication.
Sounds seem muffled or quieter; you may need to turn up volume or ask people to repeat.
Vision less sharp than before — blurry, cloudy, or with blind spots, even with glasses.
Mucus, pus, or bloody material mixed with bowel movement; may look like jelly.
Forgetting recent events, names, appointments, or getting lost in familiar places.
Things smell or taste different, reduced, absent, or distorted (e.g., metallic taste).
Feeling lightheaded, woozy, unsteady, or like you might faint; sometimes room spins (vertigo).
Seeing two images of a single object; may resolve covering one eye.
Feeling of mucus dripping from nose into back of throat; you may clear throat often.
Using illegal drugs or misusing prescription drugs despite negative consequences to health or life.
Lips feel rough, chapped, cracked, or peeling; may be from weather, dehydration, or licking.
Scalp skin sheds white flakes (dandruff), feels tight, itchy, or has dry patches.
Sharp, dull, or burning pain inside ear or around outer ear; may be worse when pulling earlobe.

symptom_name: early or late onset of menopause
explanation: Menopause before age 40 (early) or after age 55 (late); periods stop for 12 months.
Muscles around elbow suddenly tighten painfully, often with visible twitching.
Any bump near the elbow joint; common sites are tip of elbow (olecranon) or inner/outer sides.
Discomfort over elbow joint; may be on outside (tennis elbow) or inside (golfer's elbow).
Unable to fully straighten or bend elbow; feels resistant or stuck.
Visible puffiness around elbow; may look like a fluid-filled bump (bursitis).
Difficulty lifting or gripping objects due to lack of strength in elbow muscles.
Feeling sad, angry, scared, numb, overwhelmed, or having mood swings not typical for you.
Frequent, intense anger out of proportion to trigger; may lead to yelling, breaking things, or violence.
Constant hunger, eating much more than usual, or never feeling full after meals.
Unusually tall height, large hands/feet, or coarser facial features beyond family pattern.
Waking up two or more times each night to urinate; also called nocturia.
Uncomfortable, hot, or sharp sensation in eyes; often with watering or redness.
One eye points in a different direction from the other (strabismus) — in, out, up, or down.
Uncontrolled eye movements — jerking, bouncing, or drifting (nystagmus).
White part of eye looks pink or red due to dilated blood vessels; with or without discharge.
Tired, sore, or achy eyes after reading, screen use, or driving; may have headache.
Any spot, bump, scaly patch, or redness on the eyelid skin.
Upper eyelid is pulled up higher than normal, showing white above iris; may look like staring.
Puffy, swollen eyelid(s); may be one or both, upper or lower, with or without redness.
Pain in cheeks, forehead, jaw, or around eyes; can be from sinuses, nerves, or teeth.
Brief loss of consciousness with spontaneous recovery; usually due to reduced blood flow to brain.
Overwhelming lack of energy, tiredness not relieved by rest, affecting daily activities.
Intense, irrational fear of specific things (heights, spiders, crowds) causing avoidance or panic.
Sensation of coldness when others are comfortable; may be in whole body or just hands/feet.
Sensation of heat or warmth without fever; may be due to hormones, exercise, or environment.
Alternating or mixed sensations of hot and cold; common in fever, infections, or menopause.
General sense of being unwell, like coming down with something — achy, tired, queasy.
When standing or walking, toes point inward instead of straight ahead (pigeon-toed).
Body temperature above normal (usually >100.4°F or 38°C) measured orally.
Passing gas from rectum frequently or excessively; may have odor or be silent.
Group of symptoms: fever, chills, muscle aches, fatigue, headache, and sometimes cough.
Feeling of fullness, popping, or muffled hearing without infection; fluid behind eardrum.
Swelling in hands, feet, ankles, or legs due to excess fluid; skin may pit with pressure.
Sudden reddening of face, neck, or chest with warm feeling; often from emotions, alcohol, or fever.
Weakness limited to one specific body part, like one arm, one leg, or one side of face.

symptom_name: foot or toe cramps or spasms
explanation: Sudden, painful muscle tightening in arch of foot, toes, or sole; often at night.

symptom_name: foot or toe lump or mass
explanation: Any bump on foot or toes — could be bunion, ganglion cyst, corn, callus, or growth.
Pain in any part of foot — heel, arch, ball, toes; may be sharp, dull, or burning.

symptom_name: foot or toe stiffness or tightness
explanation: Difficulty bending toes or ankle; foot feels rigid, especially in morning.
Foot looks puffy, shoe feels tight, or toes appear sausage-like.
Foot drags when walking, cannot push off ground, or frequent tripping.
Feels like sand, grit, or something is in your eye, even when nothing is there.
Periods occur more often than every 21 days (fewer than 21 days between first days).
Needing to urinate more than 8 times in 24 hours; may wake you at night.
Pain focused in forehead area, above eyebrows; may feel like pressure or band.
Lump in the fold between abdomen and thigh; could be hernia, lymph node, or cyst.
Discomfort in lower abdomen where leg meets pelvis; may be sharp or dull.
Sore or tender gums; may bleed, be red, or recede from teeth.

symptom_name: hand or finger cramps or spasms
explanation: Fingers curl or hand grips involuntarily; may be painful (writer's cramp).

symptom_name: hand or finger lump or mass
explanation: Bump on palm, finger, or back of hand — like ganglion cyst, wart, or nodule.
Pain in any hand structure: joints, muscles, nerves (tingling/burning).

symptom_name: hand or finger stiffness or tightness
explanation: Hands feel tight, difficult to make a fist or fully open fingers; worse in morning.
Fingers look puffy, rings become tight, or hand looks like a balloon.
Dropping objects, weak grip, trouble pinching or opening jars.
Pain in any part of head — sharp, throbbing, dull, or pressure-like.
Burning sensation behind breastbone, often after eating or when lying down.
Soaking through pad/tampon every hour, needing double protection, or period lasting >7 days.
Coughing up blood or bloody sputum from lungs (not from stomach or mouth).
Difficulty starting urination; urine stream may be weak or stop and start.
Bump near hip bone or upper thigh; may be hernia, lipoma, or lymph node.
Discomfort in groin, outer hip, or buttock; may affect walking or weight-bearing.
Difficulty moving hip forward, backward, or rotating; common in joint issues.
Visible puffiness over hip joint or surrounding area; may be from fluid or inflammation.
Leg feels weak when lifting thigh, climbing stairs, or rising from chair.
Voice sounds rough, raspy, or lower in pitch; may strain to speak.
Acting argumentative, threatening, violent, or showing intense anger regularly.
Sudden feeling of intense heat, sweating, and reddening, especially face/chest.
Pain with each breath (pleuritic pain), often sharp, worse on deep inhale.
Outbursts of uncontrollable laughing or crying, emotional outbursts, or dramatic reactions.
Inability to get or keep an erection firm enough for satisfactory sex.
Loss of control over bowel movements — leakage or complete accidents.
Resting heart rate over 100 beats per minute; may feel pounding or fluttering.
Baby refuses to feed, arches back, falls asleep quickly, chokes, or takes very long to feed.
Baby brings up small amounts of milk after feeding; forceful vomiting or large amounts is different.
Unable to conceive after 12 months of regular unprotected intercourse (6 months if over 35).
Cycles longer than 35 days or fewer than 9 periods per year.
Difficulty falling asleep, staying asleep, or waking too early; poor sleep quality.
Bleeding or spotting between regular periods, after sex, or mid-cycle.
Leaking urine when coughing, sneezing, laughing (stress) or sudden strong urge (urge).
Nails have pits, ridges, spoon shape, thickening, discoloration, or separation from nail bed.
Scalp has patches of hair loss, scaling, redness, bumps, or flaking.
Belly button changed shape, became protruding, hard, or has discharge.
Heart feels like it skips beats, flutters, races, or beats unevenly (palpitations).
Baby cries excessively, hard to soothe, fussy, or seems in discomfort most of the time.
Uncomfortable urge to rub eyes; often with redness, watering, or gritty feeling.
Uncontrollable itch on scrotal skin; may be from jock itch, dryness, or irritation.
Unpleasant sensation that makes you want to scratch; may have rash or be invisible (pruritus).
Persistent itch around anal opening; worse at night; may be from hemorrhoids or pinworms.
Deep itch inside ear canal; may be from dryness, allergy, or infection.
Itching sensation on eyelid skin; often with redness, scaling, or swelling.
Scalp itches; may have flakes, redness, sores, or hair loss.
Yellowing of skin and whites of eyes; urine may be dark, stool pale.
Pain in jaw joint, muscles, or bone; may click, lock, or hurt when chewing.
Visible puffiness or enlargement of jaw area; may be tooth or gland related.
Pain in any joint (knee, hip, shoulder, fingers); may be aching, sharp, or throbbing.
Joint hard to move after rest; improves with movement; lasts >30 min in arthritis.
Joint looks puffy, larger than other side; may feel warm or look red.
Abnormal growth in kidney area (usually found by imaging, not self-palpable).
Sudden, painful contraction of muscles around the knee.
Bump on or around knee — could be bursitis (goose egg), cyst, or bone spurs.
Pain anywhere in knee — front, back, sides; with bending, weight, or at rest.
Difficulty fully straightening or bending knee; may feel like 'stuck.'
Knee visibly bigger than other side; may have 'water on the knee' (effusion).
Feeling that knee will buckle or give way; trouble climbing stairs or standing.
Child is much shorter than peers or growth rate slows significantly on growth chart.
Excessive tearing or watering of eyes, not from crying; may be from irritation or blockage.
Sudden, painful muscle tightening in calf, hamstring, or quadriceps.
Bump on thigh, calf, or shin; may be lipoma, cyst, varicose vein, or hematoma.
Any discomfort in leg from hip to ankle; may be muscle, bone, nerve, or vascular.
Leg muscles feel tight, limited range of motion; common in hamstrings or calves.
One or both legs larger than normal; may be pitting (leaves dent).
Leg feels heavy, hard to lift, or drags; trouble rising from chair or walking.
Painful spot on lip — cold sore (blister), ulcer (canker), or crack.
Lip(s) puffy, enlarged; may be allergic reaction (angioedema) or injury.
Menstrual bleeding lasting more than 7 days each cycle.
Numbness or feeling of 'dead' area on skin; cannot feel touch, pain, or temperature.
Lack of interest in sexual activity for months; not due to relationship or fatigue alone.
Sudden tightening of lower back muscles, often after lifting or twisting.
Pain in the lumbar spine area (below ribs, above buttocks).
Lower back feels rigid, hard to bend forward or backward; worse after rest.
Visible puffiness or mass in lower back region.
Feeling of instability or inability to support weight through lower back.
Persistent feeling of worthlessness, inadequacy, or harsh self-criticism.
Producing less than 400 ml of urine per day (about 2 cups).
Pain in belly below the navel; may be bladder, bowel, or reproductive organ related.
Pain affecting pelvis, hips, legs, or buttocks generally, not one specific spot.
Sensation of a ball or object stuck in throat when nothing is there (globus).
Any new bump in breast or armpit; can be in men too. May be tender or painless.
Visible bump on or near jawbone; could be tooth abscess, lymph node, or cyst.
Chronic swelling of arm or leg due to lymphatic fluid buildup, often after surgery.
Lump or swelling inside the scrotum — from testicle, epididymis, or hernia.
Bump on outer ear — cauliflower ear, cyst, keloid, or skin growth.
Bump on eyelid — stye (painful, red), chalazion (painless, firm).
Lump or bump on external female genitalia; may be cyst, wart, or growth.

symptom_name: mass or swelling around the anus
explanation: Bump near anal opening — hemorrhoid, abscess, or skin tag.
Black, tarry, sticky stools with foul odor, indicating digested blood from upper GI.
Dry, sticky feeling in mouth; trouble swallowing or speaking; cracked lips.
Pain inside mouth — on tongue, cheeks, gums, palate; may be burning or sharp.
Open sore inside mouth (canker sore) — round, white/yellow with red border.
Involuntary, painful muscle contractions that may cause joint to lock (contracture).
Aching or soreness in muscles; may be from overuse, flu, or medication.
Muscles feel hard, resistant to stretch; difficulty moving normally.
Muscle looks bigger or bulging compared to other side; may be from injury or inflammation.
Reduced muscle power; feels like you have to exert more to do same task.
Compulsive biting of fingernails or cuticles, often with stress or habit.
Stuffy nose; difficulty breathing through nose; may have sinus pressure.
Queasy feeling in stomach, urge to vomit, but not actually vomiting.
Sudden painful tightening of neck muscles; may cause head to turn (torticollis).
Lump on neck; could be swollen lymph node, thyroid, cyst, or growth.
Pain in neck area; may radiate to shoulders or head; worse with movement.
Difficulty turning head or touching chin to chest; feels rigid.
Visible puffiness or enlargement of neck; may affect swallowing or breathing.
Difficulty holding head up; neck feels floppy or tires easily.
Frightening dreams that wake you up; you remember details vividly.
Shape of nose changed — collapsed, deviated, hump, or saddle nose.
Blood from nostrils; may be from dryness, injury, or high blood pressure.
Unwanted repeated thoughts (obsessions) leading to repetitive actions (compulsions).
Pain with sexual activity — deep (female: cervical/uterine) or entry pain.
Any abdominal, pelvic, or back pain while pregnant; needs evaluation.
Sharp, dull, or aching sensation inside or around eye.
Soreness in gum tissue; may be red, swollen, or bleed.
Pain in one or both testicles; may be dull ache or sharp.
Pain in or around anal opening; worse with sitting or bowel movement.
Breast tenderness, heaviness, or sharp pain; may be cyclic or constant.
Cramping or pelvic pain before or during period; severe enough to limit activity.
Pressure or pain over cheeks, forehead, behind eyes; worse leaning forward.
Burning, stinging, or pain when passing urine (dysuria).
Unusually pale skin, especially inside lower eyelids, gums, or nail beds.
Feeling of heart pounding, racing, flip-flopping, or skipping beats.
Pins-and-needles, tingling, burning, or 'falling asleep' sensation.
Pain in lowest part of belly (pelvis); can be sharp, dull, or pressure.
Feeling of heaviness or pushing down in pelvis; may need to urinate often.
Fluid (white, yellow, green, or bloody) from penis, not urine or semen.
Pain in shaft, head, or base of penis; may be with erection or urination.
Red skin on penis; may be from irritation, infection, or rash.
Swelling in hands, feet, ankles, or lower legs due to fluid buildup.
Ear feels full or blocked; may be wax, fluid, or eustachian tube issue.
Excessive urine volume — more than 2.5 liters per day (10-15 cups).
Cold, numb, discolored (blue/pale) hands or feet; sores heal slowly.
After childbirth: engorgement, mastitis (red, painful, fever), or abscess.
Slouching, hunched back, forward head, or asymmetry (one shoulder higher).
Ejaculation consistently occurs within one minute of penetration, with little control.
Mood swings, irritability, anxiety, or anger in days before period; resolves after.
Any complication like bleeding, severe swelling, headache, or reduced fetal movement.
Difficulty walking, clumsiness, poor coordination, or slow movements.
Delayed, absent, or painful orgasm; or inability to reach climax.

symptom_name: problems with shape or size of breast
explanation: Significant change in breast size, shape, or symmetry; new dimpling or puckering.
Infant or child repeatedly tugging, pulling, or rubbing ears; often sign of ear pain.
One pupil larger than other; may be normal (physiologic anisocoria) or serious.
Thick yellow/green discharge from ear; indicates infection (otitis media with perforation).
Phlegm coughed up that is yellow, green, or brown; suggests bacterial infection.
Cloudy urine with white sediment; may have odor (pyuria, often UTI).
You gave birth within last 6-12 months; not current pregnancy.
Losing more than 5% of body weight in 6-12 months without trying.
Bright red blood from anus on toilet paper, in bowl, or coating stool.
Ear lobe or canal skin looks red, inflamed; sometimes hot to touch.
Red skin around nostrils or nasal tip; may be from irritation or infection.
Food or liquid comes back up into mouth without nausea or retching.
Duplicate entry. Same as above: effortless backflow of swallowed contents.
Unable to sit still; need to move, pace, or fidget; feeling internally agitated.
Unable to empty bladder completely; may strain, feel fullness, or dribble.
Pain along rib cage; worse with deep breath, coughing, or movement.
Hearing sound (ringing, buzzing, hissing) with no external source (tinnitus).
Very light periods, barely needing sanitary protection; usually less than 2 days.
Sudden uncontrolled electrical disturbance in brain: convulsions, staring, jerking.
Sudden, stabbing, or cutting pain in belly; often severe and localized.
Sudden, stabbing chest pain; may worsen with breathing or lying down.
Feeling of not getting enough air; may occur with activity or at rest.
Painful tightening of shoulder muscles; may involve neck or upper back.
Bump on shoulder — could be acromioclavicular cyst, lipoma, or muscle knot.
Pain in shoulder joint or surrounding muscles; worse with reaching or lifting.
Difficulty raising arm, reaching behind, or rotating shoulder (frozen shoulder).
Visible puffiness around shoulder joint; may be from bursitis or injury.
Difficulty lifting arm overhead or carrying objects; rotator cuff weakness.
Pain in flank (between ribs and hip); may be from kidney, muscle, or bowel.
Pressure, fullness, or stuffiness in face; worse leaning forward.

symptom_name: skin dryness, peeling, scaliness, or roughness
explanation: Skin feels rough, looks flaky, or peels like after sunburn; often itchy.
New lump, bump, or spot on skin that was not there before (wart, mole, tag).
Skin feels itchy, burning, or sore; may look red but no clear rash.
Any abnormal area on skin — sore, ulcer, blister, crust, or patch.
Brownish spots on skin; new or changing moles need monitoring.
Skin looks shiny, feels greasy, pores enlarged; often on face and scalp.

symptom_name: skin on arm or hand looks infected
explanation: Redness, swelling, warmth, pus, or spreading red streaks on upper limb.

symptom_name: skin on head or neck looks infected
explanation: Red, swollen, tender, or crusting sores on scalp, face, or neck.

symptom_name: skin on leg or foot looks infected
explanation: Red, hot, swollen leg/foot; possible drainage; may be cellulitis.
Soreness or tenderness of skin to light touch; like sunburn or nerve pain.
Area of red, bumpy, scaly, or blistered skin; may be itchy or not.
Localized puffiness of skin; may be hives (wheals) or deeper edema.
Excessive daytime drowsiness; falling asleep easily when inactive.
Getting up and walking around while asleep; no memory of it next day.
Speech sounds unclear, mumbled, or slow; may be mistaken for drunkenness.
Health issues from smoking: chronic cough, shortness of breath, wheezing.
Sudden, forceful expulsion of air through nose/mouth; often from allergy or virus.
Painful ulcer or crust inside nostril; may bleed when picking.
Pain, scratchiness, or irritation in throat; worse with swallowing.
Floaters (dark spots), cobwebs, or hazy areas moving with eye movement.
Any vaginal bleeding while pregnant; needs immediate evaluation.
Generalized feeling of rigidity in muscles and joints; whole body feels tight.
Feeling of fullness, tightness, or swelling in abdomen; may look larger.
Repetition of sounds, prolonged sounds, or blocks preventing fluent speech.
Pain just above pubic bone (lower midline belly); often bladder related.
Excessive perspiration not from heat or exercise; may be night sweats.
Scrotal sac enlarged; could be hydrocele, hernia, or orchitis.
Belly visibly larger than normal; may be from gas, fluid, or organ enlargement.
Periorbital swelling — puffy eyelids and tissues around eye.
Enlarged, tender or rubbery lumps in neck, armpit, groin; pea- to marble-sized.
Tonsils at back of throat look large, red, or have white patches.
Lingual enlargement; may cause trouble speaking, swallowing, or breathing.
Urinary frequency, urgency, pain, or blood; general bladder-related issues.
Blurred, double vision, pain, redness, discharge, or floaters.
Fussiness, poor feeding, lethargy, fever, rash, or vomiting in baby.
Frequent urination, weak stream, hesitancy, dribbling, or night urination.
Facial pain, numbness, swelling, rash, or asymmetry.
Flank pain, blood in urine, fever, swelling, or changes in urine output.

symptom_name: symptoms of the scrotum and testes
explanation: Pain, lump, swelling, heaviness, or color change in scrotum/testicles.
Frequent outbursts of anger, irritability, or tantrums out of proportion.
Abnormally strong desire to drink; persistent even after drinking.
Sensation of constriction in throat, not lump; may be anxiety or allergy.
Scratchy, tickling, or raw feeling in throat; often with dry cough.
Back of throat looks red, inflamed; may have white patches or swelling.
Feeling of fullness or visible bulge in throat; may affect breathing.
Blood from tongue surface; often from biting, injury, or ulcer.
Sores, ulcers, or growths on tongue; may be white, red, or painful.
Sore or burning sensation in tongue; may affect eating.
Hair thinning or loss (alopecia) on scalp or body beyond typical aging.
Pain in or around a tooth; may be sharp, throbbing, or constant.
BMI below 18.5; unintentional weight loss or inability to gain.
Cycles vary widely in length (e.g., 25 days, then 40 days); irregular timing.

symptom_name: unusual color or odor to urine
explanation: Urine looks dark, orange, green, blue, or smells foul, sweet, or fishy.
Excessive hair growth in areas not typical for your sex/age (hirsutism in females).
Pain in belly above navel; may be stomach, gallbladder, pancreas or liver.
Tightening and relaxing of uterine muscle; can be labor (regular) or Braxton Hicks.
Any vaginal bleeding in woman who has not had period for 12+ months.
Fluid from vagina; normal is clear/white, abnormal: green, bloody, foul, or itchy.
Lack of lubrication in vagina; may cause pain with sex or itching.
Persistent itch in vaginal area; often with discharge or irritation.
Pain in vaginal canal; may be burning, sharp, or aching.
Vaginal or vulvar skin looks red, inflamed; often with itching or discomfort.
Forceful expulsion of stomach contents through mouth.
Blood in vomit: bright red (fresh) or coffee-ground (digested).
Itching, burning, or soreness of external female genitals.
Open sore, ulcer, or blister on vulva; may be painful or painless.
Small, rough, flesh-colored growths on skin or genitals (HPV).
General or specific loss of strength; feeling of fatigue or powerlessness.
Unintentional increase in body weight; not due to diet/excess intake.
High-pitched whistling sound when breathing out; often asthma/COPD.
Thick white or yellow mucus from eye; often bacterial conjunctivitis.
Lines, creases, or sagging skin; normal aging, but early onset may be significant.
Bump on wrist; common: ganglion cyst (round, smooth, sometimes painful).
Pain in wrist joint; may be carpal tunnel, arthritis, or strain.
Difficulty bending wrist up, down, or sideways; feels tight.
Visible puffiness around wrist; may be from injury or arthritis.
Difficulty gripping, twisting (like opening a jar), or holding weight.
